---
title: "Masonry.js"
subtitle: "Quarto Extension"
author:
  - name: "Mickaël CANOUIL, _Ph.D._"
    orcid: "0000-0002-3396-4549"
    url: "https://mickael.canouil.fr"
format:
  html:
    output-file: index
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
filters:
  - masonry
masonry:
  item-selector: ".grid-item"
  gutter: 0
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <style>
        .grid-item {
          width: 160px;
          height: 120px;
          float: left;
          background: #D26;
          border: 2px solid #333;
          border-color: hsla(0, 0%, 0%, 0.5);
          border-radius: 5px;
        }
        .grid-item--width2 { width: 320px; }
        .grid-item--width3 { width: 480px; }
        .grid-item--width4 { width: 640px; }

        .grid-item--height2 { height: 200px; }
        .grid-item--height3 { height: 260px; }
        .grid-item--height4 { height: 360px; }
      </style>
---

## Using the extension

This extension provides support for [Masonry.js](https://masonry.desandro.com/).
The grids below are initialised automatically; there is no manual initialisation script.

### With the document-level defaults

A plain `.grid` is activated automatically using the document-level `masonry` defaults (`item-selector` and `gutter` here).

```{=html}
<details>
<summary>Click to see the Markdown code!</summary>
```

```{.markdown}
:::: {.grid}
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width2 .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height3}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width3}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::::
```

```{=html}
</details>
```

:::: {.grid}
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width2 .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height3}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width3}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::::

### With the friendly attributes

The `masonry-*` attributes are converted into the `data-masonry` JSON for you, inheriting the document-level `masonry` defaults for any option they do not set.

```{=html}
<details>
<summary>Click to see the Markdown code!</summary>
```

```{.markdown}
:::: {.grid masonry-percent-position="true"}
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width2 .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height3}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width3}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width2 .grid-item--height3}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::::
```

```{=html}
</details>
```

:::: {.grid masonry-percent-position="true"}
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width2 .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height3}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width3}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width2 .grid-item--height3}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::::

### Waiting for images with a timeout

The `masonry-wait-for-images-timeout` attribute caps how long the extension waits for imagesLoaded before running the layout anyway, so the grid still appears if image loading stalls.

```{=html}
<details>
<summary>Click to see the Markdown code!</summary>
```

```{.markdown}
:::: {.grid masonry-wait-for-images="true" masonry-wait-for-images-timeout="3000"}
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width2 .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height3}
:::
::::
```

```{=html}
</details>
```

:::: {.grid masonry-wait-for-images="true" masonry-wait-for-images-timeout="3000"}
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width2 .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height3}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height2}
:::
::::

### With raw `data-masonry` JSON

Writing the options yourself as raw JSON is still supported and takes precedence over the friendly attributes and metadata defaults.

```{=html}
<details>
<summary>Click to see the Markdown code!</summary>
```

```{.markdown}
:::: {.grid data-masonry='{ "itemSelector": ".grid-item", "gutter": 0 }'}
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width2 .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height3}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width3}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::::
```

```{=html}
</details>
```

:::: {.grid data-masonry='{ "itemSelector": ".grid-item", "gutter": 0 }'}
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width2 .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height3}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--height2}
:::
::: {.grid-item .grid-item--width3}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::: {.grid-item}
:::
::::
